method: multi-agent verify + adversarial falsification pass (fresh contexts); adversarial pass located the Tier-0 primary source the initial verifier missed
origin_items: data/raw/2026-07-09/techcrunch-ai.json — OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing

claims.text: OpenAI discontinuing Atlas, sunset targeted early August 2026
claims.reasoning: Initial verifier found only 3 independent Tier-2 outlets (TechCrunch, 9to5Mac, Android Authority) plus a Tier-3 origin post, sufficient under the '>=2 independent Tier<=2' path for an unsourced announcement. Adversarial pass then located an actual Tier-0 OpenAI primary source (openai.com/index/chatgpt-for-your-most-ambitious-work/) explicitly stating the Atlas sunset -- upgrading the sourcing further. Confirmed the 3 press outlets source independently (9to5Mac/Android Authority cite the James Sun X post directly; TechCrunch cites the OpenAI blog post) rather than from each other.

claims.text: Features folded into ChatGPT desktop app + Chrome extension

notes: Omitted from the published article: TechCrunch's single-source framing that the shutdown follows CEO Fidji Simo's 'side quests' cutback directive (compared to the March 2026 Sora shutdown) -- not corroborated by other outlets, so left out rather than presented as fact. Candidate source additions: 9to5mac.com, androidauthority.com (see source_candidates.json).
